name: pfSense
author: InSpectre Community
description: Integrates with pfSense via the pfSense-API package (formerly fauxapi). Discovers ARP/DHCP lease data for device inventory and blocks/unblocks devices by managing a dedicated firewall alias. Requires the pfSense-API package to be installed on your pfSense instance (System > Package Manager).

config_schema.label: pfSense Host
config_schema.help: Base URL of your pfSense web interface, e.g. https://192.168.1.1. Do not include a trailing slash.

config_schema.label: API Client ID
config_schema.help: Client ID (username) for the pfSense-API package. Configure in System > API > Keys.

config_schema.label: API Client Token
config_schema.help: Client token (password/secret) paired with the Client ID above.

config_schema.label: Block Alias Name
config_schema.help: Name of the pfSense Host alias that InSpectre will add/remove blocked IPs from. Create this alias in Firewall > Aliases (type: Host) and add a LAN block rule sourcing it before enabling blocking.

config_schema.label: Verify SSL Certificate
config_schema.help: Disable if your pfSense instance uses a self-signed certificate (default for most home installs).

get_arp_table.description: Fetch the ARP table via pfSense-API. Returns all known hosts with IP, MAC, and interface.

get_dhcp_leases.description: Fetch active DHCP leases from pfSense for hostname and MAC enrichment.

get_alias.description: Fetch the current contents of the block alias to check what is already blocked.

block_client.description: Add the device IP to the block alias. pfSense-API applies the change to the live pf table immediately.

unblock_client.description: Remove the device IP from the block alias.

test_connection.description: Verify API credentials by fetching pfSense system information.
